channel.title: leetcode on changcui
channel.description: Recent content in leetcode on changcui

item.title: 990. Satisfiability of Equality Equations
item.description: classSolution { public: vector<int> fa; int find(int x) { return x == fa[x] ? x : x = find(fa[x]); } bool equationsPossible(vector<string>& equations) { fa.resize(255); for (int i = 'a'; i <= 'z'; i++) fa[i] = i; for (auto e : equations) { auto f = e[0]; auto l = e[3]; if (e[1] == '=') fa[find(f)] = find(l); } for (auto e : equations) { if (e[1] == '!' && find(e[0]) == find(e[3])) return false; } return true; } };

item.title: 684. Redundant Connection
item.description: classSolution { public: vector<int> fa; int find(int a) { return a == fa[a] ? a : a = find(fa[a]); } vector<int> findRedundantConnection(vector<vector<int>>& edges) { auto N = edges.size(); fa.resize(N + 1); for (auto i = 1; i < N + 1; i++) { fa[i] = i; } int l, r; for (auto e : edges) { auto i_f = find(e[0]); auto j_f = find(e[1]); if (i_f != j_f) { fa[i_f] = j_f; } else { l = e[0]; r = e[1]; } } return vector<int>{l, r}; } };

item.title: 547. Friend Circles
item.description: union-find classSolution { public: vector<int> fa; int find(int x) { return x == fa[x] ? x : x = find(fa[x]); } int findCircleNum(vector<vector<int>>& M) { if (M.size() == 1) return 1; auto n = M.size(); auto ncircle = n; fa.resize(n); for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { fa[i] = i; } for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) for (int j = i; j < n; j++) { if (M[i][j] == 1) { auto i_f = find(i); auto j_f = find(j); if (i_f !

item.title: 435. Non-overlapping Intervals
item.description: greedy classSolution { public: int eraseOverlapIntervals(vector<vector<int>>& intervals) { if (intervals.size() == 0 || intervals.size() == 1) return 0; auto ans = 0; sort(intervals.begin(), intervals.end(), [](auto i, auto j){return i.front() < j.front();}); auto l = intervals[0][0]; auto r = intervals[0][1]; for (int i = 1; i< intervals.size(); i++) { if (r > intervals[i][0]) { ans++; r = min(r, intervals[i][1]); } else { l = intervals[i][0]; r = intervals[i][1]; } } return ans; } };

item.title: 56. Merge Intervals
item.description: greedy classSolution { public: vector<vector<int>> merge(vector<vector<int>>& intervals) { vector<vector<int>> result; if (intervals.size() == 0) return result; sort(intervals.begin(), intervals.end(), [](auto i, auto j) {return i.front() < j.front();}); auto left = intervals[0][0]; auto right = intervals[0][1]; for (int i = 1; i < intervals.size(); i++) { if (intervals[i][0] <= right) { right = max(right, intervals[i][1]); continue; } result.push_back(vector<int>{left, right}); left = intervals[i][0]; right = intervals[i][1]; } result.push_back(vector<int>{left, right}); return result; } };

item.title: 452. Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
item.description: greedy classSolution { public: int findMinArrowShots(vector<vector<int>>& points) { auto len = points.size(); if (len == 0) { return 0; } sort(points.begin(), points.end(), [](auto p1, auto p2) {return p1.back() < p2.back();}); auto cur = points[0][1]; auto ans = 1; for (int i = 1; i < len; i++) { if (points[i][0] <= cur) { continue; } ans += 1; cur = points[i][1]; } return ans; } };

item.title: 75. Sort Colors
item.description: classSolution { public: void sortColors(vector<int>& nums) { if (nums.size() == 0) { return; } auto len = nums.size(); for (int i = 0; i < len;) { if (nums[i] == 0) { nums.insert(nums.begin(), 0); nums.erase(nums.begin() + i + 1); i += 1; } else if (nums[i] == 2) { nums.push_back(2); nums.erase(nums.begin() + i); int j = i; while (j < len && nums[j] == 2) { j++; } if (j == len) { break; } } else { i += 1; } } } };

item.title: 155. Min Stack
item.description: priority_queue, stack classMinStack { public: /** initialize your data structure here. */ stack<int> s; priority_queue<int, vector<int>, greater<int>> p; MinStack() { } void push(int x) { s.push(x); p.push(x); } void pop() { auto e = s.top(); s.pop(); vector<int> tmp; while (!p.empty()) { auto t = p.top(); p.pop(); if (t == e) break; tmp.push_back(t); } for (auto i : tmp) { p.push(i); } } int top() { return s.top(); } int getMin() { return p.

item.title: 171. Excel Sheet Column Number
item.description: long long classSolution { public: int titleToNumber(string s) { long long rtn = 0; for (auto i : s) { rtn = 26 * rtn + i - 'A' + 1; } return rtn; } };

item.title: 13. Roman to Integer
item.description: classSolution { public: int romanToInt(string s) { auto rtn = 0; auto l = s.size(); for (int i = 0; i < l;) { if (s[i] == 'I') { if (i + 1 < l && s[i + 1] == 'V') { rtn += 4; i += 1; } else if (i + 1 < l && s[i + 1] == 'X') { rtn += 9; i += 1; } else { rtn += 1; } } else if (s[i] == 'X') { if (i + 1 < l && s[i + 1] == 'L') { rtn += 40; i += 1; } else if (i + 1 < l && s[i + 1] == 'C') { rtn += 90; i += 1; } else { rtn += 10; } } else if (s[i] == 'C') { if (i + 1 < l && s[i + 1] == 'D') { rtn += 400; i += 1; } else if (i + 1 < l && s[i + 1] == 'M') { rtn += 900; i += 1; } else { rtn += 100; } } else if (s[i] == 'V') { rtn += 5; } else if (s[i] == 'L') { rtn += 50; } else if (s[i] == 'D') { rtn += 500; } else { rtn += 1000; } i += 1; } return rtn; } };

item.title: 69. Sqrt(x)
item.description: classSolution { public: int mySqrt(int x) { return floor(sqrt(x)); } };

item.title: 125. Valid Palindrome
item.description: classSolution { public: bool isPalindrome(string s) { string x = ""; for (auto i : s) { if (isalnum(i)) { x.push_back(tolower(i)); } } auto l = x.size(); if (l == 0) return true; for (int i = 0; i < l/2; i++) { if (x[i] != x[x.size() - 1 - i]) { return false; } } return true; } };

item.title: 20. Valid Parentheses
item.description: stack.empty() classSolution { public: bool isValid(string s) { if (s.size() == 0) return true; stack<char> brs; brs.push(s[0]); for (int i = 1; i < s.size(); i++) { switch(s[i]) { case ')': if (brs.empty() || brs.top() != '(') { return false; } brs.pop(); break; case '}': if (brs.empty() || brs.top() != '{') { return false; } brs.pop(); break; case ']': if (brs.empty() || brs.top() != '[') { return false; } brs.

item.title: 66. Plus One
item.description: classSolution { public: vector<int> plusOne(vector<int>& digits) { auto carry = 1; for (int i = digits.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--) { digits[i] += carry; carry = digits[i] == 10 ? 1 : 0; digits[i] %= 10; } if (carry) digits.insert(digits.begin(), carry); return digits; } };

item.title: 160. Intersection of Two Linked Lists
item.description: nice trick /** * Definition for singly-linked list. * struct ListNode { * int val; * ListNode *next; * ListNode(int x) : val(x), next(NULL) {} * }; */ classSolution { public: ListNode *getIntersectionNode(ListNode *headA, ListNode *headB) { if (headA == NULL || headB == NULL) { return NULL; } auto p = headA; auto q = headB; while (p != q) { p = p -> next; q = q -> next; if (p == q) return p; if (p == NULL) { p = headB; } if (q == NULL) { q = headA; } } return p; } };

item.title: 53. Maximum Subarray
item.description: classSolution { public: int maxSubArray(vector<int>& nums) { if (nums.size() == 0) return 0; if (nums.size() == 1) return nums[0]; int max_ = nums[0]; vector<int> sum; sum.push_back(nums[0]); for (int i = 1; i < nums.size(); i++) { auto tmp = nums[i] + (sum[i - 1] > 0 ? sum[i - 1] : 0); sum.push_back(tmp); max_ = max(max_, tmp); } return max_; } };

item.title: 14. Longest Common Prefix
item.description: classSolution { public: string longestCommonPrefix(vector<string>& strs) { if (strs.size() == 0) return ""; if (strs.size() == 1) return strs[0]; auto min_ = 1 << 30; for (auto str : strs) { min_ = min(min_, (int)str.size()); } for (auto j = min_; j > 0; j--) { auto tmp = strs[0].substr(0, j); auto i = 1; for (; i < strs.size(); i++) { if (strs[i].substr(0, j) != tmp) break; } if (i == strs.

item.title: 28. Implement strStr()
item.description: KMP? classSolution { public: int strStr(string haystack, string needle) { if (needle.size() == 0) return 0; if (haystack.size() < needle.size()) return -1; for (int i = 0; i < haystack.size() - needle.size() + 1; i++) { if (haystack[i] == needle[0]) { int j = 1; for (; j < needle.size(); j++) { if (haystack[i + j] != needle[j]) break; } if (j == needle.size()) { return i; } } } return -1; } };

item.title: 169. Majority Element
item.description: classSolution { public: int majorityElement(vector<int>& nums) { map<int, int> cnt; for (auto num : nums) { cnt[num] += 1; if (cnt[num] > nums.size() / 2) { return num; } } return -1; } };

item.title: 202. Happy Number
item.description: classSolution { public: int helper(int n) { int rtn = 0; while (n) { auto tmp = n % 10; rtn += tmp * tmp; n = (n - tmp) / 10; } return rtn; } bool isHappy(int n) { auto tmp = helper(n); set<int> nums; nums.insert(n); while (1) { if (tmp == 1) return true; if (nums.find(tmp) != nums.end()) return false; nums.insert(tmp); tmp = helper(tmp); } return false; } };

item.title: 217. Contains Duplicate
item.description: classSolution { public: bool containsDuplicate(vector<int>& nums) { map<int, bool> n; for (auto num: nums) { if (n[num]) { return true; } n[num] = true; } return false; } };

item.title: 204. Count Primes
item.description: classSolution { public: int countPrimes(int n) { if (n <= 2) { return 0; } vector<bool> nums(n, true); auto rtn = 1; for (int i = 2;;) { for (int j = 2 * i; j < n; j += i) { nums[j] = false; } while (!nums[++i] && i < n) { } if (i >= n) break; rtn += 1; } return rtn; } };

item.title: 189. Rotate Array
item.description: classSolution { public: void rotate(vector<int>& nums, int k) { for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) { auto tmp = nums.back(); nums.pop_back(); nums.insert(nums.begin(), tmp); } } };

item.title: 190. Reverse Bits
item.description: classSolution { public: uint32_t reverseBits(uint32_t n) { uint32_t rtn = 0; for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) { rtn <<= 1; rtn |= (n >> i) & 1; } return rtn; } };

item.title: 191. Number of 1 Bits
item.description: classSolution { public: int hammingWeight(uint32_t n) { int rtn = 0; for (int i = 0; i <= 31; i++) { rtn += (n >> i) & 1; } return rtn; } };

item.title: 198. House Robber
item.description: dp classSolution { public: int rob(vector<int>& nums) { if (nums.size() == 0) { return 0; } vector<int> rob(nums.size() + 1, 0); rob[0] = 0, rob[1] = nums[0]; for (int i = 2; i <= nums.size(); i++) { rob[i] = max(rob[i - 2] + nums[i - 1], rob[i - 1]); } return rob[nums.size()]; } };

item.title: 141. Linked List Cycle
item.description: fast slow ptr /** * Definition for singly-linked list. * struct ListNode { * int val; * ListNode *next; * ListNode(int x) : val(x), next(NULL) {} * }; */ classSolution { public: bool hasCycle(ListNode *head) { if (head == NULL) { return false; } auto slow = head; auto fast = head; while (fast -> next != NULL && fast -> next -> next != NULL) { slow = slow -> next; fast = fast -> next -> next; if (slow == fast) { return true; } } return false; } };

item.title: 136. Single Number
item.description: classSolution { public: int singleNumber(vector<int>& nums) { int rtn = 0; for (auto num : nums) { rtn ^= num; } return rtn; } };

item.title: 121. Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
item.description: classSolution { public: int maxProfit(vector<int>& prices) { if (prices.size() == 0) { return 0; } int maxp = 0, minp = prices[0]; for (int i = 1; i < prices.size(); i++) { maxp = max(maxp, prices[i] - minp); minp = min(minp, prices[i]); } return maxp; } };

item.title: 118. Pascal's Triangle
item.description: classSolution { public: vector<vector<int>> generate(int numRows) { vector<vector<int>> result; for (int i = 1; i <= numRows; i++) { vector<int> cur(i, 1); if (i >= 3) { auto tmp = result.back(); for (int j = 1; j <= i - 2; j++) { cur[j] = tmp[j - 1] + tmp[j]; } } result.push_back(cur); } return result; } };

item.title: 108. Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree
item.description: /** * Definition for a binary tree node. * struct TreeNode { * int val; * TreeNode *left; * TreeNode *right; * TreeNode(int x) : val(x), left(NULL), right(NULL) {} * }; */ classSolution { public: TreeNode* helper(vector<int>& nums, int left, int right) { if (left > right) { return NULL; } else if (left == right) { return new TreeNode(nums[left]); } auto mid = (left + right) / 2; auto root = new TreeNode(nums[mid]); root->left = helper(nums, left, mid - 1); root->right = helper(nums, mid + 1, right); return root; } TreeNode* sortedArrayToBST(vector<int>& nums) { return helper(nums, 0, nums.

item.title: 104. Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
item.description: /** * Definition for a binary tree node. * struct TreeNode { * int val; * TreeNode *left; * TreeNode *right; * TreeNode(int x) : val(x), left(NULL), right(NULL) {} * }; */ classSolution { public: int helper(TreeNode* root, int depth) { if (root == NULL) { return depth; } return max(helper(root->left, depth+1), helper(root->right, depth+1)); } int maxDepth(TreeNode* root) { return helper(root, 0); } };

item.title: 101. Symmetric Tree
item.description: recursively /** * Definition for a binary tree node. * struct TreeNode { * int val; * TreeNode *left; * TreeNode *right; * TreeNode(int x) : val(x), left(NULL), right(NULL) {} * }; */ classSolution { public: bool helper(TreeNode* left, TreeNode* right) { if (left == NULL && right == NULL) { return true; } else if (left == NULL || right == NULL) { return false; } return left->val == right->val && helper(left->left, right->right) && helper(left->right, right->left); } bool isSymmetric(TreeNode* root) { if (root == NULL) { return true; } else return helper(root->left, root->right); } };

item.title: 387. First Unique Character in a String
item.description: classSolution { public: int firstUniqChar(string s) { int rep[256] = {0}; for (int i = 0; i < s.size(); i++) { rep[s[i]] += 1; } for (int i = 0; i < s.size(); i++) { if (rep[s[i]] == 1) return i; } return -1; } };

item.title: 1025. Divisor Game
item.description: dp classSolution { public: bool divisorGame(int N) { if (N == 1) return false; if (N == 2) return true; vector<bool> saved; saved.push_back(false); saved.push_back(true); for (int i = 3; i <= N; i++) { bool res = false; for (int j = 1; j * j < i; j++) { if (i % j == 0 && !saved[i - j - 1]) { res = true; break; } } saved.

item.title: 100. Same Tree
item.description: /** * Definition for a binary tree node. * struct TreeNode { * int val; * TreeNode *left; * TreeNode *right; * TreeNode(int x) : val(x), left(NULL), right(NULL) {} * }; */ classSolution { public: bool isSameTree(TreeNode* p, TreeNode* q) { if (p == NULL && q == NULL) return true; else if (p == NULL || q == NULL) return false; return (p->val == q->val) && isSameTree(p->left, q->left) && isSameTree(p->right, q->right); } };

item.title: 455. Assign Cookies
item.description: classSolution { public: int findContentChildren(vector<int>& g, vector<int>& s) { if (g.empty() || s.empty()) return 0; sort(g.begin(), g.end()); sort(s.begin(), s.end()); int rtn = 0; for (auto i = s.back();;) { auto j = g.back(); while (i < j) { g.pop_back(); if (g.empty()) break; j = g.back(); } if (!g.empty()) { rtn += 1; s.pop_back(); g.pop_back(); if (s.empty() || g.empty()) break; i = s.back(); } else { break; } } return rtn; } };

item.title: 350. Intersection of Two Arrays II
item.description: classSolution { public: vector<int> intersect(vector<int>& nums1, vector<int>& nums2) { map<int, int> cnt; for (auto num: nums1) { cnt[num] += 1; } vector<int> rtn; for (auto num: nums2) { auto it = cnt.find(num); if (it == cnt.end() || it -> second == 0) continue; rtn.push_back(num); it -> second -= 1; } return rtn; } };

item.title: 326. Power of Three
item.description: classSolution { public: bool isPowerOfThree(int n) { return n > 0 ? !(1162261467 % n) : 0; } };

item.title: 268. Missing Number
item.description: classSolution { public: int missingNumber(vector<int>& nums) { auto len = nums.size(); auto sum = 0; for (auto num : nums) { sum += num; } return (len + 1) * len / 2 - sum; } };

item.title: 242. Valid Anagram
item.description: classSolution { public: bool isAnagram(string s, string t) { int cnts[255] = {0}; int cntt[255] = {0}; for (auto i : s) cnts[i] += 1; for (auto i : t) cntt[i] += 1; for (auto i = 'a'; i <= 'z'; i++) { if (cnts[i] != cntt[i]) return false; } return true; } };

item.title: 237. Delete Node in a Linked List
item.description: stupid problem /** * Definition for singly-linked list. * struct ListNode { * int val; * ListNode *next; * ListNode(int x) : val(x), next(NULL) {} * }; */ classSolution { public: void deleteNode(ListNode* node) { *node = *node -> next; } };

item.title: 234. Palindrome Linked List
item.description: /** * Definition for singly-linked list. * struct ListNode { * int val; * ListNode *next; * ListNode(int x) : val(x), next(NULL) {} * }; */ classSolution { public: ListNode* reverseList(ListNode* head) { ListNode* pre = NULL; while (head != NULL) { auto tmp = head->next; head->next = pre; pre = head; head = tmp; } return pre; } bool isPalindrome(ListNode* head) { if (head == NULL || head->next == NULL) return true; ListNode* fast = head; ListNode* slow = head; while (fast->next !

item.title: 206. Reverse Linked List
item.description: iteratively /** * Definition for singly-linked list. * struct ListNode { * int val; * ListNode *next; * ListNode(int x) : val(x), next(NULL) {} * }; */ classSolution { public: ListNode* reverseList(ListNode* head) { ListNode* pre = NULL; while (head != NULL) { auto tmp = head->next; head->next = pre; pre = head; head = tmp; } return pre; } }; recursively classSolution { public: ListNode* reverseList(ListNode* head) { if (head == NULL || head -> next == NULL) return head; auto tmp = reverseList(head->next); head->next->next = head; head->next = NULL; return tmp; } };

item.title: 1078. Occurrences After Bigram
item.description: c++ does not have split class Solution(object): def findOcurrences(self, text, first, second): """ :type text: str :type first: str :type second: str :rtype: List[str] """ words = text.split(' ') rtn = [] for i in range(len(words)-2): tmp = words[i:i+3] if first == tmp[0] and second == tmp[1]: rtn.append(tmp[2]) return rtn

item.title: 1170. Compare Strings by Frequency of the Smallest Character
item.description: classSolution { public: int f(string s) { auto rtn = 1; auto cur = s[0]; for (int i = 1; i < s.size(); i++) { if (s[i] < cur) { rtn = 1; cur = s[i]; } else if (s[i] == cur) { rtn += 1; } } return rtn; } vector<int> numSmallerByFrequency(vector<string>& queries, vector<string>& words) { vector<int> wa(11, 0); for (auto w : words) wa[f(w)] += 1; vector<int> rtn; for (auto q : queries) { auto tmp = 0; for (int i = f(q) + 1; i <= 10; i++) tmp += wa[i]; rtn.

item.title: 1160. Find Words That Can Be Formed by Characters
item.description: vector faster than map classSolution { public: int countCharacters(vector<string>& words, string chars) { vector<int> cnt(255, 0); for (auto c : chars) { cnt[c] += 1; } auto rtn = 0; for (auto word : words) { auto tmp = cnt; auto flag = true; for (auto c : word) { if (tmp[c] == 0) { flag = false; break; } else tmp[c] -= 1; } rtn += flag ? word.

item.title: 1154. Day of the Year
item.description: classSolution { public: bool isLeap(int year) { if (year % 100 == 0) return year % 400 == 0; else return year % 4 == 0; } int dayOfYear(string date) { auto year = stoi(date.substr(0, 4)); auto month = stoi(date.substr(5, 2)); auto day = stoi(date.substr(8)); vector<int> days{31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31}; if (isLeap(year)) days[1] = 29; auto rtn = 0; for (int i = 1; i < month; i++) rtn += days[i - 1]; rtn += day; return rtn; } };

item.title: 1137. N-th Tribonacci Number
item.description: classSolution { public: int tribonacci(int n) { int tri[38]; tri[0] = 0; tri[1] = 1; tri[2] = 1; for (int i = 3; i <= n; i++) { tri[i] = tri[i - 1] + tri[i - 2] + tri[i - 3]; } return tri[n]; } };

item.title: 1128. Number of Equivalent Domino Pairs
item.description: classSolution { public: int numEquivDominoPairs(vector<vector<int>>& dominoes) { int cnt[10][10] = {0}; for (auto dom : dominoes) { cnt[dom[0]][dom[1]] += 1; } int rtn = 0; for (int i = 1; i <= 9; i++) { for (int j = i; j <= 9; j++) { if (i == j) { auto tmp = cnt[i][j]; if (tmp >= 2) { rtn += tmp * (tmp - 1) / 2; } } else { auto tmp = (cnt[i][j] + cnt[j][i]); if (tmp >= 2) { rtn += tmp * (tmp - 1) /2; } } } } return rtn; } };

item.title: 1122. Relative Sort Array
item.description: classSolution { public: vector<int> relativeSortArray(vector<int>& arr1, vector<int>& arr2) { vector<bool> first(1001, false); for (auto i : arr2) { first[i] = true; } auto len = arr1.size(); for (int i = 0; i < len;) { auto rt = find(arr2.begin(), arr2.end(), arr1[i]); if (rt != arr2.end()) { if (first[arr1[i]]) { first[arr1[i]] = false; } else { arr2.insert(rt, arr1[i]); } arr1.erase(arr1.begin() + i); len -= 1; } else { i += 1; } } sort(arr1.

item.title: 1114. Print in Order
item.description: classFoo { public: bool mutex1, mutex2; Foo() { mutex1 = true; mutex2 = true; } void first(function<void()> printFirst) { // printFirst() outputs "first". Do not change or remove this line. printFirst(); this->mutex1 = false; } void second(function<void()> printSecond) { while (this->mutex1) {} // printSecond() outputs "second". Do not change or remove this line. printSecond(); this->mutex2 = false; } void third(function<void()> printThird) { while (this->mutex1 | this->mutex2) {} // printThird() outputs "third".

item.title: 1108. Defanging an IP Address
item.description: insert classSolution { public: string defangIPaddr(string address) { for (int i = 0; i < address.size(); i++) { if (address[i] == '.') { address.insert(address.begin() + i, '['); address.insert(address.begin() + i + 2, ']'); i += 2; } } return address; } };

item.title: 1103. Distribute Candies to People
item.description: math classSolution { public: vector<int> distributeCandies(int candies, int num_people) { int epoch = 0; for (; ; epoch++) { int needed = (epoch + 1) * num_people * ((epoch + 1) * num_people + 1) / 2; if (candies <= needed) break; } vector<int> result(num_people, 0); for (int i = 1; i <= num_people; i++) { result[i - 1] += epoch * i + num_people * epoch * (epoch - 1) / 2; } candies -= epoch * num_people * (epoch * num_people + 1) / 2; for (int i = 1; i <= num_people; i++) { int needed = i + epoch * num_people; if (candies <= needed) { result[i - 1] += candies; break; } else { result[i - 1] += needed; candies -= needed; } } return result; } };

item.title: 1089. Duplicate Zeros
item.description: if (i + 1 < len) classSolution { public: void duplicateZeros(vector<int>& arr) { auto len = arr.size(); for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) { if (arr[i] == 0) { for (int j = len - 2; j >= i + 1; j--) { arr[j + 1] = arr[j]; } if (i + 1 < len) arr[i + 1] = 0; i += 1; } } } }; vector classSolution { public: void duplicateZeros(vector<int>& arr) { auto len = arr.

item.title: 1051. Height Checker
item.description: sort classSolution { public: int heightChecker(vector<int>& heights) { auto sorted = heights; sort(sorted.begin(), sorted.end()); auto result = 0; for (int i = 0; i < heights.size(); i++) { if (sorted[i] != heights[i]) { result++; } } return result; } };

item.title: 1047. Remove All Adjacent Duplicates In String
item.description: stack: classSolution { public: string removeDuplicates(string S) { string result = ""; stack<char> chars; for (auto c : S) { if (chars.empty() || c != chars.top()) { chars.push(c); } else { chars.pop(); } } while (!chars.empty()) { result += chars.top(); chars.pop(); } reverse(result.begin(), result.end()); return result; } }; string(faster): classSolution { public: string removeDuplicates(string S) { string result = ""; for (auto c : S) { if (result.empty() || result.
